title: 2002 Super League Grand Final
wikitext:
{{Short description|British rugby league championship match}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox rugby league football match |year = 2002 |title = Super League Grand Final |image = |imagesize = 90px |alt = 2002 Super League logo |home = [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] |away = [[Bradford Bulls]] |home_abbr = ST H {{leagueicon|St Helens|16}} |away_abbr = BRA {{leagueicon|Bradford Bulls|16}} |home_half1 = 12 |home_half2 = 7 |home_total = 19 |away_half1 = 8 |away_half2 = 10 |away_total = 18 |date = 19 October 2002 |stadium = [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]] |location = {{flagicon|ENG}} Manchester |MoM_title = [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] |MoM = {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Paul Deacon]], ({{leagueicon|Bradford|16}} [[Bradford Bulls]]) |anthem_title = [[And did those feet in ancient time|Jerusalem]] |anthem = |referee = [[Russell Smith (referee)|Russell Smith]] |attendance = 61,138 |network = [[Sky Sports]] |commentators = [[Eddie Hemmings (rugby league)|Eddie Hemmings]] |commentators2= [[Mike Stephenson]] |commentators3= |tournaments = [[Super League Grand Final]] |last = [[2001 Super League Grand Final|2001]] |next = [[2003 Super League Grand Final|2003]] }} The '''2002 Super League Grand Final''' was the '''Fifth official Grand Final''' and conclusive and championship-deciding game of [[Super League VII]]. Held on Saturday 19 October 2002 at [[Old Trafford (football ground)|Old Trafford]], Manchester, the game was played between [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] and [[Bradford Bulls]]. Refereed by Russell Smith, the match was seen by a crowd of 61,138 and was won by St Helens 19 - 18. ==Background== {{Main|Super League VII}} {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Position"| !width=175 |Team ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Played"|Pld ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Won"|W ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Drawn"|D ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Lost"|L ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Points for"|PF ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Points against"|PA ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Points difference"|PD ! style="width:20px;" abbr="Points"|Pts |- |1||style="text-align:left;"|[[Image:Saintscolours.svg|16x16px]] [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] |28||23||0||5||927||522||+405||'''46''' |- |2||style="text-align:left;"|[[Image:Bullscolours.svg|16x16px]] [[Bradford Bulls]] |28||23||0||5||910||519||+391||'''46''' |} ===Route to the Final=== ====St Helens==== St Helens, by finishing top of the table automatically qualified for the play-off semi-final where they were drawn at home to Bradford. A surprising loss saw Saints have to go the long route to the grand final by playing bitter rivals [[Wigan Warriors]] in the elimination semi-final; a match they won 24–8 to set up another game against Bradford. {| class="wikitable plainrowheaders" style="text-align:center;margin-left:1em;" |+ St Helens !width="25" | Round !width="150" | Opposition !width="60" | Score |- !scope=row style="text-align:center"| Semi Final | {{leagueicon|bradford|16}} [[Bradford Bulls]] (H) |bgcolor=FFDDDD| 26–28 |- !scope=row style="text-align:center"| Elimination Semi-Final | {{leagueicon|Wigan|16}} [[Wigan Warriors]] (H) |bgcolor=DDFFDD| 24–8 |- | colspan="3" style="background-color:white;"| '''Key:''' (H) = Home venue; (A) = Away venue. |} ====Bradford Bulls==== Bradford as the team finishing second in the season also qualified for the play-off semi-final where they had to travel to St Helens. A close fought game saw Bradford win 28–26 to go straight through to the grand final. ==Match details== {{rugbyleaguebox | bg = #eeeeee | date = 19 October 2002 | team1 = [[Bradford Bulls]] [[Image:Bullscolours.svg|16px]]| score = 18 - 19| report = [https://web.archive.org/web/20030512073421/http://www1.superleague.co.uk/reports/index.asp?fixid=231719 Report] | team2 = [[Image:Saintscolours.svg|16px]] [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] | points1 = '''Tries:''' [[Scott Naylor]]
